data.title: Linde upgraded at JPM, 'built better for current market conditions than many materials companies'
data.description:
Linde (LIN) +1.4% in Friday's trading as J.P. Morgan upgraded shares to Overweight from Neutral with a $525 price target, raised from $455, saying the world's top industrial gas supplier is "built better for current market conditions than many materials companies."

Linde’s (LIN) industrial customers in the U.S. likely will increase operating rates given the opportunity to export at higher prices and capture expanded margins, and its refinery customers also should boost operating rates, JPM analyst Jeffrey Zekauskas said, noting the 3-2-1 crack spread has widened from ~$20 to $40 as oil prices have jumped.

The oversupplied helium market, which previously was a drag on Linde's (LIN) price growth, also has tightened as QatarEnergy temporarily suspended its LNG operations, Zekauskas said, adding that very little helium business is done at spot and the shorter-term effects on the company probably are minimal.

Linde (LIN) also tends to increase prices at a faster rate in an inflationary market than it does in a non-inflationary market, Zekauskas said.

The analyst had downgraded the stock just five weeks ago, foreseeing valuation risk in a slow demand environment with pockets of price weakness, but "opportunities for volume growth in the U.S. and Europe coupled with some stabilization in the rare gas and helium market should at least offset some volume penalties in Asia and Europe and the less positive currency translation effects."

data.title: Cathie Wood’s once upon a time $1,500 Zoom call by 2026 meets reality as software struggles
data.description:
Nearly four years after Cathie Wood made one of the most eye-catching forecasts of the pandemic-era technology boom, shares of Zoom Communications (ZM) remain far from the lofty levels her firm once envisioned.

Back on June 9, 2022, Wood’s investment firm ARK Invest published open-source research suggesting Zoom’s share price "could approach $1,500, compounding at a 76% annual growth rate, in 2026." At the time, the company’s stock closed that day at $110.40. 

ARK’s analysis also laid out an even wider valuation range. The firm wrote that by 2026, “our bull and bear cases are $2,000 and $700 per share, respectively,” for Zoom. Regarding the bullish scenario, ARK added: “We believe that there is a 25% probability that Zoom could be worth $2,000 per share or more in 2026.” 

The forecast came as investors were still digesting the aftermath of the massive surge in technology stocks during the COVID-19 pandemic, when demand for remote communication tools sent Zoom’s shares sharply higher.

In the weeks following ARK’s report, Zoom briefly traded higher, reaching an intraday peak of $124.05 on July 8, 2022. However, momentum quickly faded. By the end of that summer the stock had slipped below $100 per share and has not returned above that level since. 

Today, Zoom trades near $74.11, leaving the stock down roughly 32.6% from where it stood when ARK issued its forecast. 

See Ark Invest's 2022 investment note on Zoom Communications here.

With 2026 still in its early months, Zoom would need a spectacular rally to have any shot at Wood’s once-lofty targets, especially as software stocks continue to struggle.

Below are Cathie Wood’s five actively managed ETFs:

  • ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK)
  • ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) 
  • ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ)
  • ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) 
  • ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (ARKF)
  • ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX)

data.title: Vista Gold outlines Mt Todd's $1.1B NPV5 and targets engineering start in 2027 amid expanded funding
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Vista Gold Corp. (VGZ) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Frederick H. Earnest, President and CEO, highlighted 2025 as a pivotal year marked by the completion of the Mt Todd feasibility study, surpassing four years without a workplace incident, maintaining zero reportable environmental events, and ending the year with a strong treasury. The CEO stated, "In November, we surpassed 4 years without a workplace incident and maintained 0 reportable environmental events and ended the year with a strong treasury."
  • Earnest announced the closing of a $44.85 million public offering, emphasizing, "We are committed to prioritizing the efficient use of our cash and creating value for our shareholders through disciplined execution of our strategy for the Mt Todd gold project."
  • Douglas Tobler, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "Vista ended 2025 with cash on hand of $13.6 million and has recently completed an equity offering with net proceeds of $41.9 million. Securing these funds comes at a very important time because it provides Vista with immediate ability to fund the programs that Fred will mention later in this discussion. We are now well positioned from a financial perspective to execute on our objective to begin detailed engineering in 2027. We also continue to have a clean balance sheet with no debt."

Outlook

  • The company plans to commence detailed engineering and design for Mt Todd in 2027, following completion of technical studies and permitting activities throughout 2026. Management stated, "Completing this work is an important prerequisite to initiating detailed engineering and design, which we expect to begin in 2027."
  • The team is expanding in Australia, with the recent hiring of an approvals manager and two executive general managers in Perth, and further appointments anticipated.
  • Vista anticipates the decision to begin detailed engineering and design will mark the start of an approximately 27-month period culminating in first gold production.

Financial Results

  • Vista reported a net loss of $7.5 million for the year ended December 31, 2025. This compares to net income of $11.2 million for 2024, with the swing attributed to a $16.9 million gain in 2024 from a royalty interest grant, $1.9 million in capitalized development costs in 2024, and $800,000 in proceeds from equipment sales that year.
  • Cash on hand at year-end was $13.6 million, further bolstered by $41.9 million in net proceeds from the recent equity offering.
  • Mt Todd-related expenditures totaled $5.6 million in 2025, up from $3.5 million in 2024, while corporate administration expenses were steady at $3.6 million versus $3.7 million the prior year.
  • The company maintains a debt-free balance sheet.

Q&A

  • Unknown Analyst (on behalf of Heiko Ihle, H.C. Wainwright): Asked about discount rates for projects in safe jurisdictions. Douglas Tobler responded, "I think what you're going to see is for projects like Mt Todd sitting in the Northern Territory, which is really a Tier 1 jurisdiction. I think discount rates will be better. I think cost of debt will be better as we look to finance the project."
  • Unknown Analyst: Inquired about input cost changes amid high gold prices and potential concerns with construction costs. Frederick H. Earnest answered, "While the gold price has gone up in the last 9 months significantly, so far and with the exception of the last week or so in Australia, fuel prices have remained pretty constant... Certainly, it's reasonable to expect some modest increase year-on-year in equipment costs and what will ultimately be the construction costs of the project. But at this point in time, we don't see any real significant changes that would dissuade our views on Mt Todd."
  • Unknown Attendee (private investor): Questioned Vista's strategy regarding mining Mt Todd independently versus partnership and timing for first gold production. Earnest stated, "What we've disclosed publicly is that looking at where we're at with the permit modification process that we expect that, that will occur in 2027. I think our best estimate today would be that we'll be in a position to make that decision mid-2027. So if you want to add 27 months on to that date, that will give you a rough estimate of when we would expect to see first gold poured."
  • Unknown Attendee (private investor): Asked about financing outlook and dilution concerns. Tobler explained, "Early numbers would tell us that something in the order of 65% to 70% of the project can be financed, I'll call it debt, but levered. And then the balance of that would have to be equity. So yes, there will be more shares out, but we're very focused on making sure the outcome of that on a per share basis is actually accretive, not dilutive."
  • Unknown Attendee: Asked about Sun Valley Gold's share activity. Earnest clarified, "Sun Valley Gold Fund... has been converted to a family office and that managed funds have been redistributed to individual investors or may have been sold. This was not necessarily Sun Valley Gold selling shares because they no longer liked Vista Gold, but rather Sun Valley Gold Fund winding down and redistributing."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was generally neutral, with questions focused on project financing, cost inflation, and strategic direction, without notable skepticism or pressing negativity.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive outlook in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing project leverage to gold prices, strong balance sheet, and prudent execution. Statements such as "we are confident that this is the right market environment in which to advance Mt Todd" underscored this.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment from both analysts and management remains consistently confident, with a slightly increased emphasis on execution risk and financing structure in the current quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company advanced from feasibility study completion to active permit modification and technical studies, with a clear timeline for commencing detailed engineering in 2027.
  • Management's language has shifted from exploring strategic options—including partnerships—to firming up plans for independent development and team expansion in Australia.
  • Key financial metrics shifted from a quarterly net loss of $723,000 (Q3) to a full-year net loss of $7.5 million, reflecting the absence of large one-time gains seen in 2024.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from partner interest and confidentiality agreements in Q3 to more granular concerns about financing structure, dilution, and project timeline in Q4.
  • Management's confidence increased around project readiness and funding, with more specific hiring and execution plans articulated.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing monitoring of input and construction costs, particularly relating to fuel and global energy prices, as a potential area of concern, but does not see "any real significant changes that would dissuade our views on Mt Todd."
  • Analysts raised concerns over potential dilution from future equity financing; management pledged a focus on accretive outcomes for shareholders.
  • Geopolitical events and their impact on discount rates and financing were discussed, but Mt Todd's location in a "Tier 1 jurisdiction" was presented as a mitigating factor.

Final Takeaway

Vista Gold management emphasized that 2025 marked a transformative year with the Mt Todd project's feasibility study, strengthened funding, and concrete steps toward independent project development. The company’s forward progress includes detailed engineering targeted for 2027, supported by recent equity financing, a growing management team, and ongoing permit modifications. Management remains confident in the project's economics and leveraged exposure to gold prices, positioning Mt Todd as a highly attractive development-stage opportunity in the current market environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Phillips 66 tops growth factor grades among S&P energy holdings
data.description:
The S&P 500 has experienced a challenging two-week period marked by escalating geopolitical tensions, sector rotation into defensives, and technical deterioration.

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 S&P energy holdings arranged according to their growth factor grade, a quantitative measure used to evaluate expansion trajectory.

The list is topped by Phillips 66 (PSX), with a growth grade of B+. The Williams Companies (WMB) and Valero Energy (VLO) follow, with Baker Hughes (BKR) and Chevron (CVX) rounding out the top five. Phillips 66 (PSX) stands alone in “Buy” territory with its B+ grade, while the remaining stocks transition into more moderate growth profiles.

Other major industry players including Exxon Mobil (XOM) and ConocoPhillips (COP) appear further down the list with C-range grades. The bottom of the rankings includes Kinder Morgan (KMI), EOG Resources (EOG), and SLB N.V. (SLB), all carrying D grades that indicate potential growth headwinds.

The growth factor grade is a quantitative assessment used in stock analysis to evaluate a company’s growth prospects and expansion trajectory. This metric systematically analyzes multiple growth-related indicators to determine how effectively a company is scaling its business operations.

Growth factor grades are typically expressed on a letter scale (A+ through F), where higher grades indicate stronger growth characteristics relative to sector peers. An A+ grade represents exceptional growth potential, while lower grades suggest more moderate expansion or potential headwinds.

Here is the list:

  1. Phillips 66 (PSX), Growth grade: B+

  2. The Williams Companies, Inc. (WMB), Growth grade: B

  3. Valero Energy Corporation (VLO), Growth grade: B-

  4. Baker Hughes Company (BKR), Growth grade: C

  5. Chevron Corporation (CVX), Growth grade: C

  6. Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM), Growth grade: C

  7. ConocoPhillips (COP), Growth grade: C-

  8. Kinder Morgan, Inc. (KMI), Growth grade: D

  9. EOG Resources, Inc. (EOG), Growth grade: D

  10. SLB N.V. (SLB), Growth grade: D

data.title: BofA's Hartnett sees troubling echoes of 2008 in today's markets
data.description:
Bank of America strategist Michael Hartnett is sounding the alarm that today’s market conditions bear an unsettling resemblance to the period leading up to the global financial crisis.

In a note to clients, Hartnett pointed to surging oil (CL1:COM) (CO1:COM) (USO) prices and mounting concerns around private credit (VPC) (HYIN) (BIZD) as key indicators that “asset performance in 2026 is more ominously close to price action seen from mid’07 to mid’08.”

The Iran war, which erupted February 28, has pushed oil prices more than 60% higher this year. Hartnett drew parallels to 2007-2008, when oil doubled to $140 a barrel from $70, coinciding with the “subprime tremors” that brought down Northern Rock and Bear Stearns.

Meanwhile, worries are growing about banks’ (KBE) (KBWB) exposure to private credit, an asset class facing fund redemptions, scrutiny of underwriting standards, and concerns about artificial intelligence’s (AIEQ)  impact on borrowers.

Hartnett cautioned that an ECB rate hike in July 2008—the same day oil peaked—proved to be “one of the greatest policy mistakes of all time,” forcing the central bank to cut by 325 basis points just 74 days later as Lehman Brothers collapsed.

For now, market consensus assumes the Iran conflict will be short-lived and private credit issues aren’t systemic, encouraging bullish positioning as investors bet that “policymakers always ride to Wall Street rescue.”

Hartnett recommended fading oil above $100 per barrel and the U.S. dollar (DXY) above 100, in addition to buying 30-year U.S. Treasuries (US30Y) above 5% and the S&P 500 (SP500) under 6.6K. At the time of writing, the 30-year tenor was at 4.91%, DXY stood at 100.3 and the S&P 500 changed hands at 6.64K.

Market-tracking funds: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Tyler Technologies to repurchase up to $200M in shares
data.description:
• Tyler Technologies (TYL) announced repurchase of up to $200M of its common stock.
  • Share repurchases under the plan may begin March 16, 2026, and run through April 30, 2026.
  • The plan operates under the company’s $1.0B share buyback authorization approved by its board on Feb. 3, 2026.
  • The new authorization replaced all previous buyback programs dating back to October 2002.
  • As of March 13, 2026, about $734.4M remains available under the repurchase authorization.
  • Buybacks are expected to be funded through existing cash and borrowings under the company’s credit facility.

data.title: Consumer staples will lose pricing power as energy costs rise – analyst
data.description:
Consumer staple (XLP) companies are poised to lose their pricing power as rising energy costs from Middle East tensions squeeze the sector, according to Nik Modi, RBC Capital Markets co-head of global consumer and retail research.

In an interview with CNBC, Modi warned that the sector’s recent defensive rally was built on shaky fundamentals, and the conflict’s economic fallout will pressure both margins and volumes across the industry.

The sector is up 9.4% year-to-date, although it is down about -0.1% on the week.

While consumers are feeling the pinch of higher gas prices and home energy bills immediately, food price increases may take longer to materialize due to corporate hedging strategies that typically extend six to nine months.

“If this sustains, they’re going to go up,” Modi said, noting that the Middle East accounts for 35%-40% of global fertilizer exports. However, even if input costs rise, companies will have far less room to pass those costs along to already-pressured consumers.

Packaged food stocks (SP500-30202030) are particularly vulnerable in this environment because they are already experiencing declining sales volumes.

Modi explained that these companies face “less pricing latitude” than in prior inflationary cycles, putting them squarely “in the crosshairs.”

Names like J. M. Smucker Co. (SJM), Newell Brands (NWL), and Traeger (COOK) also face additional margin pressure from rising logistics and shipping costs.

Major multinational corporations, including PepsiCo (PEP), Mondelez International (MDLZ), Coca-Cola (KO), and Procter & Gamble (PG) face specific risks due to their exposure to the Middle East region.

“There’s going to be a lot less latitude to take pricing than what we’ve seen in prior cycles where this has happened,” Modi cautioned, highlighting how the conflict disrupts supply chains for companies reliant on international trade routes.

Despite the broad sector weakness, Modi identified two names he believes are better positioned: Primo Brands (PRMB) and Church & Dwight (CHD).

Primo Brands (PRMB), the largest home and office water delivery business in the U.S., benefits from being entirely domestic and could actually gain if imported products face disruptions. Church & Dwight (CHD) appeals due to its strong value portfolio and “dry powder” available for potential acquisitions.

Modi noted that the sector’s strong start to the year was driven more by investors fleeing the AI trade into defensive positions rather than improving fundamentals. With the recent selloff erasing those gains, he sees a challenging road ahead for the entire space.

“It’s really going to be kind of a stock picker's game in this sector for this year,” he concluded.

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS).

data.title: VAALCO outlines 2026 production guidance and major drilling milestones as portfolio diversification accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: VAALCO Energy, Inc. (EGY) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO George Maxwell highlighted that “over the past 3 years, we have delivered outstanding operational and financial results, including generating over $750 million in adjusted EBITDAX while meeting or exceeding our quarterly guidance targets.” Maxwell announced the divestment of all Canadian assets and the expansion in Cote d'Ivoire, having been “named operator with a 60% working interest in the Kossipo field on Block CI-40.” He underscored the ongoing FPSO refurbishment at Baobab and significant development drilling planned for later in 2026, with at least one well expected on full production by year-end. Maxwell stated, “We have accomplished many things in these past 5 years, growing VAALCO from a single asset delivering around 5,000 barrels a day to a diversified multi-country operator, well on our way to achieving our goal of 50,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day.”
  • CFO Ronald Bain reported, “we delivered 17,452 net revenue interest barrels of oil equivalent per day of sales in 2025, above the high end of our increased guidance. We also delivered production of 16,556 net revenue interest barrel of oil equivalent per day or 21,160 working interest barrels of oil equivalent per day, both above the midpoint of VAALCO's increased guidance.” Bain explained the net loss for the quarter was “driven primarily by a noncash impairment charge of $67.2 million due to the sale of our Canadian assets.”

Outlook

  • Bain outlined guidance for 2026, forecasting Q1 production between 18,700 and 20,600 working interest barrels of oil equivalent per day and full year production between 20,100 and 22,400 working interest barrels of oil equivalent per day. He noted, “We expect both to increase materially in the second half of 2026 when the FPSO is back online and the full impact of the Gabon drilling campaign is realized.” Guidance for CapEx in 2026 is projected between $290 million and $360 million, with significant expenditure in Gabon, completion of the FPSO refurbishment, and drilling at Baobab. Sales volumes for the full year 2026 are expected to be between 14,900 and 18,050 barrels of oil equivalent per day.

Financial Results

  • Bain stated, “These strong sales numbers helped us generate adjusted EBITDAX of $173.4 million and net cash from operating activities of $212.7 million for the full year of 2025.” Net loss for Q4 was $58.6 million or ($0.56) per diluted share, resulting in a full year net loss of $41.4 million, attributed mainly to noncash impairment from the Canadian asset sale. Production costs for 2025 were $158 million, at $24.89 per barrel. Unrestricted cash at year-end was $58.9 million. Cash G&A costs remained below the low end of guidance. Bain noted, “Collections from the Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation accelerated in 2025, and all of our aged receivables are now current,” with EGPC outstanding accounts receivable reduced from $113 million to $31 million by year-end. The company paid $26.5 million in dividends in 2025.

Q&A

  • Stephane Foucaud, Auctus Advisors: Asked for CapEx breakouts in Cote d'Ivoire and timing of major spending. Maxwell responded, “the majority of that is split between the drilling program in Gabon and the hookup for the FPSO in Cote d'Ivoire... the CapEx is kind of limited to just looking at preparation and development of the field development plan for submission. So that's really a limited amount of around $10 million.” On Kossipo, Maxwell said large CapEx would be a 2028 event after field development plan approval.
  • Jeffrey Robertson, Water Tower Research: Asked about Brent price assumptions and PSC mechanics. Bain replied, “Underlying Brent assumptions that we assumed for 2026 was a baseline of $65, per Brent.” On working interest strategy, Maxwell stated, “right now, we're more than comfortable at our 60% working interest and operatorship, and we've got an excellent relationship with our partner, PetroCI.”
  • Chris Wheaton, Stifel: Sought CapEx details for FPSO and drilling. Maxwell estimated “around about $50 million of that would be our share between the hookup and the recommissioning,” with the balance for completions. On free cash flow, Bain prioritized using excess cash to “not draw as much” from credit facilities rather than increase returns to shareholders in the near term.
  • Charlie Sharp, Canaccord: Asked about CapEx delta and spillover into 2027. Bain observed FPSO rebuild costs “roughly about $80 million to $100 million higher than it was originally planned,” but no rollover of the Gabon drilling program into 2027 is expected.
  • William Dezellem, Tieton Capital Management: Asked about pricing advantages from West Africa operations. Maxwell noted, “our routes to monetize the crude in the export markets remain uninhibited by that particular activity and that conflict,” with no additional price advantage seen yet. On Egypt exploration, COO Thor Pruckl said, “we were a bit surprised, I guess, at the volumes that came in with that well... the rates have sustained themselves quite high.”
  • Aaron Schafer: Asked about realized prices for the quarter. Bain reported, “Gabon, it was about $58; Egypt, $54; and Canada, $53.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was generally direct, with several pressing for clarity on CapEx timing, project breakdowns, and production guidance, reflecting a neutral to slightly negative sentiment as they sought specifics on risk, cash flow allocation, and project execution.
  • Management maintained a confident and detailed tone in prepared remarks, but responses in Q&A occasionally reflected caution, especially regarding CapEx flexibility and project timing. Maxwell emphasized, “I think we've demonstrated the success of our ability to manage and work with our partners,” and Bain regularly assured that the company would meet guidance and maintain operational discipline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment shifted from steady confidence to a more cautious optimism, with management providing more granular breakdowns and acknowledging cost increases and project delays.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more detailed, with explicit production and CapEx ranges provided, compared to a broader outline in Q3. The company now expects a material production uplift in the second half of 2026, whereas in the previous quarter, the uplift was projected for 2026 and into 2027.
  • Strategic focus shifted more clearly to Cote d'Ivoire and Gabon, with the Canadian asset sale completed and capital redirected to core assets and high-ROI drilling programs. The FPSO project in Cote d'Ivoire is now close to relaunching, while in Q3 it was still undergoing refurbishment.
  • Analysts in Q4 were more focused on CapEx composition, timing, and the implications of project delays and cost increases, reflecting heightened scrutiny on execution risk and financial discipline.
  • Key metrics such as production, sales, and adjusted EBITDAX increased since Q3, but the quarter closed with a net loss due to a noncash impairment not present in the previous quarter.
  • Management's tone evolved from steady confidence in Q3 to a more measured confidence, acknowledging cost overruns and timing uncertainties but reiterating commitment to guidance and shareholder value.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the transitional nature of 2025, with production downtime in Cote d'Ivoire due to the FPSO project and drilling in Gabon starting late in Q4.
  • Noncash impairment from the Canadian asset sale drove a net loss in the quarter.
  • CapEx overruns on the FPSO refurbishment and timing delays for drilling campaigns were acknowledged, with Maxwell stating, “Costs have increased in relation to the amount of steel work predominantly on that vessel.”
  • Analysts raised concerns over project execution, CapEx spillovers, and the ability to manage multiple simultaneous major projects.
  • Bain addressed cash flow risks by noting, “we would use that cash to not draw on the facility,” and highlighted ongoing hedging to manage price risk.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that VAALCO’s portfolio transformation is delivering tangible results, citing operational outperformance, the successful sale of Canadian assets, and significant progress on major projects in Cote d'Ivoire, Gabon, and Egypt. The company forecasts a ramp-up in production and cash flow from H2 2026 as new drilling and FPSO projects come online. Management reiterated its commitment to operational efficiency, prudent investment, and continued shareholder returns, while noting that cost discipline and execution of upcoming drilling campaigns are key to unlocking further value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Southwest Airlines ends service to Chicago's O'Hare and Washington's Dulles airports
data.description:
'Southwest Airlines (LUV) announced on Friday it is ending service to Chicago’s O’Hare International Airport and Dulles Airport in Washington D.C., effective June 4, as part of the carrier’s ongoing effort to refine its network.

Service into the Chicago and Baltimore/Washington area will continue with service to Chicago’s Midway Airport, Washington’s Reagan National, and Baltimore/Washington International Airport.

Although the carrier did not give a specific reason for the change, it said operating at Chicago O’Hare “continues to be challenging,” while it also remains committed to serving the “important Washington area.” 

All affected employees will have the opportunity to bid for open positions across the Southwest Airlines' (LUV) network, including positions at Midway and Reagan National.

Travelers can keep their existing reservations for travel on or before June 3 but must rebook or travel standby for travel after June 4 or request a refund for the unused ticket and any ancillary travel charges (extra legroom, priority boarding, etc.).

As the conflict in the Middle East continues to drive up the price of fuel, Southwest (LUV) shares—along with the majority of the airline sector (JETS)—are set to close in the red for a third consecutive week.

data.title: Innovation Beverage Group prices $6M public offering
data.description:
• Innovation Beverage Group (NASDAQ: IBG) said on Friday it had announced the pricing of a public offering, with expected gross proceeds of approximately $6M.
  • The offering had consisted of 3.43 million common units (or pre-funded units), each including one ordinary share or one pre-funded warrant, one Series A registered common warrant, and one Series B registered common warrant.
  • Each warrant had been exercisable at $1.75 per share, with a public offering price of $1.75 per common unit.
  • The Series A and Series B common warrants had been exercisable immediately and were set to expire 60 months after the initial issuance date.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about March 16, 2026.
  • The company had expected to use $2.5 million of the proceeds for a non-interest-bearing loan to Blockfuel to exercise a call right under a Dec. 24, 2025, securities purchase agreement, with the remainder for general corporate purposes and working capital.
  • Aegis Capital Corp. is acting as the exclusive placement agent for the offering. 
  • Shares -52.44%.
